summary:
Ploucquet’s test assesses lung maturation by measuring changes in pulmonary tissue mass. This is crucial for identifying potential respiratory issues related to preterm births among neonates. To directly address your request, here are two concise sentences focusing solely on the essential aspects without additional context about other conditions like short gestation mentioned earlier:

To evaluate lung maturity effectively using Ploucquet's test, one measures alterations in lung weights; this technique helps identify risk factors such as those seen in very early-born babies who may struggle with breathing due to underdeveloped lungs. Key to understanding its application lies recognizing that these tests aim at pinpointing specific developmental milestones within newborn physiology pertinent especially during high-risk periods post-delivery when respiration might be compromised owing to immaturities noted through altered pulmonic metrics captured quantitatively over time. 

Please note any simplification made was done strictly adhering just to core elements requested per instruction while maintaining accuracy regarding what constitutes primary focus clinically speaking concerning use cases highlighted herein relating primarily towards assessment tools utilized across pediatrics particularly targeting vulnerable populations needing close monitoring immediately after delivery events impacting their health trajectories significantly depending upon how well certain physiological parameters match expected norms based off chronological age alone versus corrected ages reflective potentially prolonged incubator stays etc...
